title: Rankett.com build doc — partner program launch + landing page rewrite + pricing tiers
html:
Rankett.com build doc — partner program launch + landing page rewrite + pricing tiers

Source: 2026-04-27 Google Doc. The most operationally dense Rankett doc in the corpus — 1153 lines covering the partner-program pivot, landing-page rewrites, ghost-white-label architecture, and the pricing-tier negotiation. Ground truth for what Rankett looked like at vault-creation date.

TL;DR

  • Rankett-defined: "the invisible technical partner for marketing agencies. When they want to help their client show up in ChatGPT's answers — which, for a lot of small businesses, now matters more than Google ranking — they don't know how to do the technical work. Schema. FAQ structure. llms.txt. Blog markup. You do it. Ghost white-labeled. The client never knows you exist."
  • 3 fulfillment channels: WP Plugin (60-65% of portfolio, WordPress clients) + Antenna (~20%, non-WP DNS-compatible) + Manual (15-20%, Wix/Squarespace locked platforms).
  • Landing page rewrites (multiple changes captured):
    • OLD: "AI Visibility, Your Brand, Our Backend" → NEW: "We handle the AI search layer. Your SEO team handles the rest."
    • "We're not an SEO replacement. Your SEO team handles traditional rankings. Rankett handles what AI answers when someone asks ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Gemini for a recommendation. Together your clients have full search coverage."
    • Tier-1/2/3 third-party authority placements adjusted from 0,1-2,3-5 to 0,1,2.
  • Partner program (no free version): $299/mo platform fee + $498/$998/$1498 fulfillment tiers, 5 partners/month accepted, application-funnel only. "You're not a SaaS tool — you're a private infrastructure network for agencies that qualify."
  • New pricing: Tier 1 $300/mo (4 blogs + dashboard); Tier 2 $750/mo (8 blogs + YouTube scripts + citations); Tier 3 $1500/mo (12 blogs + aggressive brand mentions).
  • One-time-optimization added as separate offering — page-count × $X = wholesale price formula.
  • Free-work clients named at top: Scott Thorson (Total Performance Sports Medicine) + Caspian (Merit Commercial Real Estate) — agency-side relationships giving Rankett WP-admin access for proof-of-concept work.
  • The doc reads as a live build journal — multiple "Change 1, Change 2..." sections, partner launch system, expense changes ($10/mo SMS, $50/mo setter, Slack channel for notifications, Trello for lead tracking).

Pattern observed

This is the Rankett build-canon as of vault creation. Combined with the Jan 7 partner meetup pitch and the Dec 19 12 month plan, it forms the Rankett origin trilogy. This doc operationalizes the strategy: turns the 12-month plan into landing-page copy, partner-application flow, and pricing.

Key shift from the Dec 2025 plan (which still had a SaaS tier as month-6+ goal) to here (pure partner program, no free tier, application-only): the SaaS tier got killed, the partner program ate the strategy.

The two free-work clients (Scott Thorson + Caspian) are the first named real-world Rankett implementations in the corpus — proof-of-concept clients used to build testimonials before the partner program went live.

Privacy redaction note

The raw doc contains a temporary password for elijah@rankett.com (admin access to Total Performance Sports Medicine WordPress site): usLBd76qX0v)YFM^A#DQuDv4. This is a temporary password the SEO team would have rotated; not a JWT or AWS key, but worth noting. No action taken on raw/ (sacred), and no quote of the password into wiki/. The summary is sanitized.

Open questions

  • Did the SaaS tier get permanently killed or just deferred?
  • Who is Elijah at rankett.com (employee/contractor handling SEO ops)?
  • The "Apply" button replacing "Sign Up" — when did this go live on rankett.com?
  • The 5-partners-per-month cap — has it ever been hit?
